'Table of Contents'

memes.label: Arctic Circle
memes.description: The parallel of latitude at approximately 66°33' N, marking the southern boundary of the Arctic region where the phenomena of the midnight sun and polar night occur.

memes.label: "Arctic Exploration"
memes.description: "The broader historical enterprise of exploring the Arctic regions, the context in which Lieutenant Hobson's expedition takes place and to which the novel contributes its fictional narrative."

memes.label: "Arctic Ocean"
memes.description: "The vast frozen sea that forms the novel's ultimate horizon — both the destination of the Hudson's Bay Company expedition and the relentless force that shapes the fate of every character who ventures onto its ice."

memes.label: Arctic Wildlife
memes.description: "The rich fauna of the Arctic regions — including polar bears, reindeer, musk oxen, foxes, beavers, and countless waterfowl — that sustained the colonists and provided the economic foundation of the Hudson's Bay Company's fur trade."

memes.label: Aurora Borealis
memes.description: "The spectacular luminous phenomenon of the polar skies, a defining feature of the Arctic environment in the novel, observed by the colonists during the long polar night and described with both scientific curiosity and poetic wonder."

memes.label: "Bering Sea"
memes.description: "The Pacific gateway to the Arctic Ocean, whose waters — relatively free of ice for much of the year — represent the colonists' best hope of resupply and, ultimately, their route to safety."

memes.label: "Cape Bathurst"
memes.description: "A prominent headland on the Arctic coast of North America above the 70th parallel, chosen by Lieutenant Hobson as the site for Fort Hope — a decision that proves fateful when the cape is later discovered to be attached not to the mainland but to a drifting ice island."

memes.label: Cape Eskimo
memes.description: "A prominent cape on Victoria Island's eastern coast, named by the colonists after the Eskimo family encamped there, serving as a key navigational landmark and the site of significant wildlife observation and geological changes during the island's drift."

memes.label: Cape Krusenstern
memes.description: "A cape on the Arctic coast of North America, marking the northern turn of the coastline beyond the mouth of the Coppermine River, named after the Russian explorer Adam Johann von Krusenstern."

memes.label: "Cape Michael"
memes.description: "A cape on Victoria Island (later revealed to be an ice island) at the opposite end from Cape Bathurst, named by Lieutenant Hobson's party during their reconnaissance of the drifting landmass."

memes.label: Captain Back
memes.description: A real historical Arctic explorer (1796–1878) whose 1834 expedition discovered King William's Land and who is referenced in The Fur Country as a precursor to the novel's events.

memes.label: "Captain Craventy"
memes.description: "The commanding officer at Fort Reliance, who hosts the farewell soirée for the Arctic expedition and provides Lieutenant Hobson with his marching orders from the Hudson's Bay Company."

memes.label: Captain Hatteras
memes.description: A referenced Arctic explorer, the protagonist of Jules Verne's earlier novel The Adventures of Captain Hatteras (1866), whose discovery of a collared fox is recalled in The Fur Country.

memes.label: Coppermine River
memes.description: "A major Arctic river flowing from south to north into Coronation Gulf, discovered by Samuel Hearne in 1772, and serving as the recommended route for Lieutenant Hobson's expedition to reach the northern coast of North America."

memes.label: Coronation Gulf
memes.description: "A vast Arctic estuary at the mouth of the Coppermine River, dotted with the Duke of York Archipelago, marking the gateway to the Polar Sea for Lieutenant Hobson's expedition to the northern coast of North America."

memes.label: "Corporal Joliffe"
memes.description: "The bustling, well-meaning 'Corporal in charge of details' at Fort Hope, whose boundless energy is only matched by his incompetence — a comic figure whose wife quietly runs everything he claims to manage."

memes.label: Dog Sledging
memes.description: "The primary mode of Arctic winter transportation in the novel, employing teams of six or more dogs to pull sledges across the frozen terrain — the essential technology that made the Hudson's Bay Company's far-northern operations and Hobson's expedition possible."

memes.label: Earthquake of January 8
memes.description: "A violent earthquake on January 8 that struck Cape Bathurst and severed the isthmus connecting Victoria Land to the North American continent, transforming it into a drifting ice-island — the central geological catastrophe of the novel."

memes.label: "Fort Confidence"
memes.description: "A Hudson's Bay Company post at the northern extremity of the Great Bear Lake, serving as the final staging point for Lieutenant Hobson's expedition before it pushes into uncharted territory toward the Arctic coast."

memes.label: "Fort Enterprise"
memes.description: "A Hudson's Bay Company trading post on the route from Fort Reliance to the Great Bear Lake, serving as an intermediate waypoint for Lieutenant Hobson's Arctic expedition."

memes.label: Fort Franklin
memes.description: "A Hudson's Bay Company fort on the southern shore of the Great Bear Lake, named after the Arctic explorer Sir John Franklin, providing access to the lake's navigable waters during summer and ice routes in winter."

memes.label: "Fort Hope"
memes.description: "The Hudson's Bay Company trading post and settlement established by Lieutenant Jaspar Hobson near the Arctic coast, which is later discovered to be situated on a drifting ice island."

memes.label: Fort Providence
memes.description: "A Hudson's Bay Company fort on the northern shore of the Great Slave Lake, serving as a trading post and waypoint for fur convoys and expeditions travelling between the Company's southern and northern territories."

memes.label: Fort Reliance
memes.description: A Hudson's Bay Company trading post in the Canadian Arctic, the starting point of the novel's expedition to establish a new fort on the Arctic coast.

memes.label: Founding of Fort Hope
memes.description: "The establishment of Fort Hope on Cape Bathurst in July 1859 by Lieutenant Hobson's expedition — the Hudson's Bay Company's newest and northernmost factory, built on what was believed to be solid continental ground but later revealed to be the ice-foundation of a drifting island."

memes.label: "Sir John Franklin"
memes.description: "The celebrated British Arctic explorer whose tragic disappearance and death haunt the novel's imaginative landscape, serving as a constant reminder of what the polar regions can take as well as give."

memes.label: Fur Trade
memes.description: The commercial exchange of animal furs and pelts, the economic foundation of the Hudson's Bay Company's operations in the Canadian Arctic.

memes.label: Great Bear Lake
memes.description: "A vast Arctic lake beyond the 61st parallel, comparable in significance to a Mediterranean Sea of the north, serving as a crucial hub for the Hudson's Bay Company's fur-trading operations and the staging ground for Lieutenant Hobson's northern expedition."

memes.label: Great Slave Lake
memes.description: "One of the largest lakes beyond the 61st parallel, 250 miles long by 50 across, situated between the Hudson's Bay Company's southern and northern trading territories, serving as the departure point for Lieutenant Hobson's Arctic expedition."

memes.label: Hobson's Northern Expedition
memes.description: "Lieutenant Jaspar Hobson's Hudson's Bay Company expedition of 1859, tasked with exploring the northern shores of the Great Bear Lake, establishing a new fort on the Arctic coast, and escorting the astronomer Thomas Black to observe the total solar eclipse of 1860."

memes.label: "Hudson's Bay Company"
memes.description: "The historic British fur-trading corporation that governs the vast territories of Rupert's Land and employs the novel's central characters in their Arctic expedition."

memes.label: Ice Field
memes.description: A vast expanse of pack ice and frozen sea that dominates the Arctic landscape in The Fur Country, shaping travel, survival, and the fate of Fort Hope.

memes.label: Iceberg
memes.description: "Massive formations of polar ice central to the geography and fate of Victoria Island — the colonists' home was built upon a colossal ice-field disguised as solid earth, and icebergs constantly threatened their survival in the Arctic Ocean."

memes.label: "Lieutenant Jaspar Hobson"
memes.description: "A forty-year-old officer of the Hudson's Bay Company, of Irish descent, tasked with leading an expedition to establish a new fort near the Arctic coast."

memes.label: "Kalumah"
memes.description: "A young Eskimo girl who befriends Paulina Barnett and the colonists at Fort Hope, and who — months after her departure — crosses two hundred miles of Arctic ice to find her European friends stranded on the drifting ice island, in one of the novel's most remarkable acts of loyalty."

memes.label: "Kellet"
memes.description: "A soldier of the Hudson's Bay Company and one of Mac-Nab's carpenter's men, whose remembered pickaxe proves unexpectedly vital during the colonists' final struggle for survival."

memes.label: "Michael Mac-Nab"
memes.description: "A Scottish soldier and master carpenter in the Hudson's Bay Company service, one of the ten men selected by Captain Craventy for Lieutenant Hobson's Arctic expedition, responsible for all construction at Fort Hope."

memes.label: "Madge"
memes.description: "The faithful servant and devoted companion of Paulina Barnett, who accompanies the celebrated traveller on the Arctic expedition to Fort Hope."

memes.label: "Marbre"
memes.description: "A skilled hunter, trapper, and soldier in the Hudson's Bay Company service, one of the two expert hunters (with Sabine) chosen for Lieutenant Hobson's Arctic expedition, whose tracking abilities and wilderness knowledge prove essential to the colonists' survival."

memes.label: "Midnight Sun"
memes.description: "The natural phenomenon of continuous daylight during the Arctic summer, observed and described by the colonists of Fort Hope in their first season beyond the Arctic Circle."

memes.label: "Mrs. Joliffe"
memes.description: "The capable and sharp-eyed wife of Corporal Joliffe, who runs the domestic affairs of Fort Hope with a firm hand, and is widely acknowledged as the real authority behind her bustling husband."

memes.label: "Norman"
memes.description: "An experienced old boatman of the Hudson's Bay Company, who pilots Lieutenant Hobson and Paulina Barnett across the Great Bear Lake through a violent storm, demonstrating the steady seamanship that only decades on northern waters can confer."

memes.label: Northwest Passage
memes.description: The sea route connecting the Atlantic and Pacific Oceans through the Arctic Archipelago of North America, which serves as a powerful thematic backdrop in The Fur Country.

memes.label: "Paulina Barnett"
memes.description: "A celebrated English traveller and the novel's most distinguished guest, who joins the Hudson's Bay Company expedition to the Arctic coast in search of new discoveries, bringing scientific curiosity and indomitable courage to Fort Hope."

memes.label: Permafrost
memes.description: The permanently frozen subsoil of the Arctic regions, an omnipresent environmental constraint that shapes every aspect of construction, travel, and survival in The Fur Country.

memes.label: "Petersen"
memes.description: "A Canadian soldier of the Hudson's Bay Company, one of Mac-Nab's carpenter's men who helps build Fort Hope, a reliable scout and hunter who participated in the walrus hunt and later volunteered for a dangerous ice-field expedition — twice singled out by the narrator as 'the Canadian.'"

memes.label: "Polar Bear Encounters"
memes.description: "The dangerous encounters with polar bears near Fort Hope, which present one of the most immediate threats to the colonists' survival in the Arctic wilderness."

memes.label: Polar Night
memes.description: The period during winter months when the sun remains continuously below the horizon in polar regions, a central element of the colonists' ordeal at Fort Hope in Jules Verne's The Fur Country.

memes.label: "Rae"
memes.description: "The blacksmith of Fort Hope, one of the bravest men in the colony, whose skill with iron and forge proves indispensable to the survival of the Arctic settlement."

memes.label: "Royal Geographical Society"
memes.description: "The London-based learned society that provides Paulina Barnett's credentials as a celebrated traveller and explorer in Jules Verne's The Fur Country."

memes.label: Russian Fur Company
memes.description: "The Russian-controlled fur-trading operations in North America, whose establishments were leased by the Hudson's Bay Company in 1842 at an annual rent of £40,000, extending the Company's reach to the Pacific coast and Bering Strait region."

memes.label: "Sabine"
memes.description: "A skilled hunter of the Hudson's Bay Company, partnered with Marbre, whose steady marksmanship and intimate knowledge of the Arctic wilderness make him one of the expedition's most valuable scouts."

memes.label: Scurvy
memes.description: "The potentially fatal vitamin C deficiency disease that posed a constant threat to Arctic expeditions, and the preventive measures — including scurvy-grass, lime juice, and sorrel — that Lieutenant Hobson stocked at Fort Hope to protect his colonists."

memes.label: Sergeant Felton
memes.description: A sergeant stationed at Fort Confidence on Great Bear Lake, brother-in-law of Sergeant Long, who assists Jaspar Hobson's expedition during their stopover at the fort.

memes.label: Sergeant Loin
memes.description: A sergeant stationed at Fort Reliance who assists in reviving the frozen astronomer Thomas Black upon his dramatic arrival at the fort.

memes.label: "Sergeant Long"
memes.description: "A fifty-year-old veteran soldier of the Hudson's Bay Company, constitutionally brave and unwaveringly loyal, who serves as Lieutenant Jaspar Hobson's second-in-command on the Arctic expedition."

memes.label: Sir Roderick Murchison
memes.description: A real historical geologist and President of the Royal Geographical Society, referenced in The Fur Country as the ultimate authority on Arctic maps.

memes.label: Solar Eclipse of 1860
memes.description: "A total solar eclipse on July 18, 1860, visible across the Arctic regions of North America, Spain, and North Africa — the scientific observation of this eclipse was the primary mission of the astronomer Thomas Black and a catalyst for the entire northern expedition."

memes.label: St. Louis Fur Company
memes.description: "The American St. Louis Fur Company, the principal rival of the Hudson's Bay Company in the North American fur trade, operating from the United States and competing for pelts and territory across the continent."

memes.label: "The Ice Island"
memes.description: "The catastrophic discovery that Fort Hope is built not on solid ground but on a vast drifting ice island, and the subsequent journey southward toward the Bering Sea."

memes.label: "Thomas Black"
memes.description: "An eccentric British astronomer who joins the Arctic expedition to observe a solar eclipse, and whose scientific obsessions provide both comic relief and genuine scientific observation in the novel."

memes.label: Victoria Island
memes.description: "An immense floating ice-island, originally thought to be a peninsula attached to the North American continent, discovered by Lieutenant Jaspar Hobson's expedition and later revealed to be a drifting sheet of ice carrying Fort Hope and its colonists across the Arctic Ocean."

memes.label: Washburn Bay
memes.description: "A deep bay on the eastern coast of Victoria Island, forming part of the isthmus boundary that once connected the peninsula to the North American mainland, and a key encampment point during Hobson's exploration circuit."
